folio.label: 86r
folio.l: Et incipit fabula

note.notetxt: 1untill here all this fragment is copyed with a brown ink. Double check.ODR

folio.l: Lordynggã there is , in ©orkshire as I gesse
A mersshy contre , called holdernesse
In whicú there wente , a lymytour a boute
To päche and eke to begge , it is no doute
And so byfel , that on a day this frere
Had preched at a chirche , in his manere
And specially , a bouen euäy thynge
Excited he the peeple , in his prechynge
To trentales , and to ©euen for goddis sake
Where witú men mygút , holy houses make
There as diuine seruyse , is honoured
Nat there as it is wasted , and deuoured
Ne there it nedetú nat / forfor to be ©eue
As to possessioneres , that moun lyue
Thanked be god , in wele and habundaunce
Trentals q¶ he , delyueren fro penaunce
Here frendes soules , as wel old as ©onge
ña , whan that they ben , hastily I songe
Nat for toholden a prest , Ioly and gay
He syngetú nat , but oo masse on a day
Delyueretú out a non q¶ he , the soules
Ful hard it is , w flesshoke , or with owles
To ben I clawed , or to brenne or bake
Now spede ©ow hastily , for cristes sake
And whan this frere , had seid al his entente
Witú Qui cum patre , fortú his weye he wente
¢ Whan~e folk in chirche , had ©euen hi~ what hem list
He went his wey , no lengere wold he rest
Witú skrippe and tipped staf , I tukked heye
In euäy hous , he gan to poore and prye
And begged mele and chese , or ellis corn~
His felawe had a staf , typped witú horn~
A peyre of tables , al of yuory
And a poyntel , polysshed fetisly
And wrot the names alwey , as he stode
Of alle folk , that ©af hem any good
A scaunce that he , wold , for hem preye
ñeue vs a busshel whete , malt or reye
